title: Cannot run podman on fresh RHEL 8.9 installation as a non-root user
body:
### Issue Description

I have a brand new RHEL 8.9 minimal install and trying to run podman but it is failing due to a newuidmap error.
```
$ podman version
ERRO[0000] running `/usr/bin/newuidmap 1630 0 1000 1 1 100000 65536`: newuidmap: open of uid_map failed: Permission denied 
Error: cannot set up namespace using "/usr/bin/newuidmap": exit status 1
```
I followed the (semi-related) instructions here: https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7006710 without effect.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install RHEL 8.9 Server minimal install. Include the Container Tools
2. SSH to the server
3. Run `podman version`


### Describe the results you received

```
ERRO[0000] running `/usr/bin/newuidmap 1630 0 1000 1 1 100000 65536`: newuidmap: open of uid_map failed: Permission denied 
Error: cannot set up namespace using "/usr/bin/newuidmap": exit status 1
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expected podman to display the version number

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

```
$ cat /etc/redhat-release 
Red Hat Enterprise Linux release 8.9 (Ootpa)
```

### Installed packages:
[installed_packages.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/14912479/installed_packages.txt)

### cap info
```
$ getcap  /usr/bin/new*idmap
/usr/bin/newgidmap cap_setgid=ep
/usr/bin/newuidmap cap_setuid=ep
```


### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: I suggest you reach out to the Red Hat support with RHEL issues
